description_long: This is a small, single-story cabin with a simple rectangular footprint and a dark brown gable roof. The exterior walls are light-colored, likely finished in wood or painted siding, and the roof appears to be made of shingles or similar material. There are a few small, square windows symmetrically placed on the visible facades, providing natural light to the interior. A single door is present on one of the longer sides, positioned slightly off-center. The cabin sits on a flat, circular base, suggesting it is intended as a standalone structure. The overall design is minimalistic and functional, suitable for use as a compact residential or recreational building.
description: A small, light-colored rectangular cabin with a dark gable roof, minimal windows, and a single door, set on a circular base.
description_view_0: The cabin is seen from an angle showing the gable roof and one side with a small window and the main door.
description_view_1: This view highlights the longer facade with the door and a window, showing the rectangular shape and roof symmetry.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the cabin is visible, showing the gable end and a single window, with the roof's slope prominent.
description_view_3: A direct view of the longer facade with the door and window, emphasizing the cabin's simple, symmetrical design.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate pitch, covered in dark shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: Two small, square windows symmetrically placed on opposite facades, single window per visible side
attribute_door_placement: Single exterior door on the longer facade, slightly off-center
